stemHtml: You are about to take-off from an airfield at mean sea level. There is a Nimbostratus at 5000 ft. At ground level, the temperature is 3ºC and it is 7ºC at the top of the cloud. In the vicinity there is a mountain ridge which you are planning to cross. Which of the following conditions will result in an icing hazard?

options.a: Freezing level below the cloud and a temperature of -4ºC at 2 000 ft, with continuous rain.
options.b: Probability of icing increases when dry snow starts to fall from the cloud.
options.c: Icing is unlikely under these conditions.
options.d: Turbulence in the windward side of the ridge.
